name: Stage 2
description: Aggressive market-level tune (~520 Nm / 230 PS, +30% torque / +41% power). REQUIRES hardware modifications: intercooler upgrade is MANDATORY at this boost level. Strongly recommended: 3-inch turbo-back exhaust, decat downpipe. Stock turbo (Garrett GT2052V) operates at the edge of its safe map - reduce highway full-load duration. Injectors should be in healthy condition (verified spray pattern). NOT for stock OEM hardware in original condition.

edits.comment: Peak torque band (1700-2500 rpm). Targets 9750 raw cap (~520 Nm). Cells already at 7500 stock saturate at cap; cell 8 lifts proportionally (+40%).
Mid-upper RPM (2700-3300 rpm). +30% with 9750 cap. Wide torque shelf - feels like a much bigger engine on hills and overtakes.
Upper RPM (3500-4100 rpm). +20% on the power-band ceiling. Real torque here is set by injection x boost combination.
Pre-rev-limit (4300 rpm). +10% gentle lift - keeps top-end smooth without flat-spot.

+20% boost target at peak band. Cap 2750 mbar absolute (~1.75 bar relative) - the practical upper limit for the GT2052V before turbo overspeed. INTERCOOLER UPGRADE MANDATORY at this boost; stock IC heat-soaks above ~2400 mbar.
Lower boost lift in upper RPM band to protect turbo from overspeed - VNT vanes closing too aggressively at high airflow damages thrust bearings.

+18% rail pressure (rows 3..15, cols 8..15). Cap 14500 raw (~1450 bar) is the practical limit for the stock CP1 high-pressure pump. Higher pressure improves atomisation under +28% fuel quantity, reducing smoke.

+28% main fueling (rows 5..15, cols 9..15). Wider RPM range than Stage 1 to support peak power lift. Cap 9500 prevents smoke-limit excursions on poor fuel batches. Verify injector condition before applying.
+18% on Boost x RPM injection map at high boost x high RPM. This is the secondary fueling layer that scales with actual measured boost - critical for clean peak power delivery. Cap 9500.
+18% transient fueling, broad load range. Reduces turbo lag dramatically - essential for Stage 2 driveability.
+25% on secondary acceleration map. Matched to main map's broader scaling for Stage 2 power.

options.label: EGR off (MANDATORY for Stage 2)
options.description: Software EGR delete via phase-of-injection saturation. ABSOLUTELY REQUIRED at Stage 2 fueling levels - the additional fuel produces significantly more soot, and EGR-on operation will clog the intake manifold and EGR valve within months. No hardware blanking plate required (ECU-only delete).

edits.comment: Flatten phase-of-injection map to its factory maximum to disengage EGR loop.
